title: Data Table Grouping by Taxonomy Column
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to group data table rows by taxonomy (e.g., Line of Business, Topic), so that I can organize and view metrics by business categories.
description:
Add taxonomy-based row grouping to the data dictionary data table.

### UX Design
- Dropdown above the table to select a taxonomy
- Table re-renders with row grouping by selected taxonomy value
- Default taxonomies: Line of Business and Topic (extensible data model for future taxonomies)

### Multi-Taxonomy Handling
When a metric belongs to multiple taxonomy values, the metric appears once under each group (duplicate row per taxonomy value). This was preferred over establishing a "primary" taxonomy.

### Technical Approach
- Uses jQuery DataTables row grouping plugin
- SQL join produces duplicate rows for metrics with multiple taxonomy assignments
- Taxonomy dropdown is dynamic — new customer-added taxonomies appear automatically

### Known Issue
Selecting a taxonomy then reverting to "Select Taxonomy" placeholder could produce odd grouping. This was fixed.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy dropdown renders above the data table
Selecting a taxonomy groups rows by that taxonomy's values
Metrics with multiple taxonomy values appear in each relevant group
New customer-created taxonomies appear in the dropdown automatically
Reverting to default selection clears grouping cleanly
ux_changes: Taxonomy dropdown above data table. Row grouping by selected taxonomy. Metrics appear under each assigned taxonomy group.
middleware_changes: SQL join returns duplicate metric rows per taxonomy value. Dynamic taxonomy list query.
data_model_changes: Data model designed to be extensible for future taxonomy types beyond Line of Business and Topic.
implementation_notes: jQuery DataTables row grouping plugin used. Fixed a bug where deselecting taxonomy produced incorrect grouping.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the taxonomy dropdown and grouping. Ryan tested and found a bug when reverting to default selection, which Waqar fixed. Both confirmed the multi-taxonomy duplicate-row approach was the desired behavior.
